response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the candidate has roughly 18-19 years of professional software development (2006-present), clearing the 15-year minimum. Step 2: Education — holds a BS in Computer Science from MIT, satisfying the bachelor's requirement; no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field. Step 3: Seniority match — career trajectory is Senior/Staff individual contributor on product engineering teams; there is no evidence of director-level scope, organization-wide technical strategy, or advising strategic customers/partners. Step 4: AI expertise — recent work at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling, which is relevant but recent (since 2025) and tooling-oriented rather than recognized, demonstrated AI product leadership. Step 5: Cloud expertise — practical experience with AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, and high-throughput microservices, but at implementation level rather than platform-strategy level. Step 6: Entrepreneurial signal — founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) since 2016, plus conference talks and workshops, showing initiative and knowledge sharing. Overall: meets the minimums and shows craftsmanship, breadth, and some emerging AI exposure, but falls well short of the preferred profile of a recognized AI expert operating at director-level strategic scope.
strengths: 18+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform, plus active community teaching and conference talks
Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic developer tooling
concerns: No director/principal-level experience setting technical strategy or advising executives and strategic customers
AI experience is recent and tooling-focused, with no recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products at scale
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited evidence of cloud platform strategy beyond implementation work

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs director-level IC role
Depth of AI expertise and recognition
Long, broad engineering experience with entrepreneurial initiative

response.justification: The candidate comfortably meets the tenure and education minimums and brings genuine breadth, craftsmanship, and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling, along with founder experience that fits the entrepreneurial preference. However, this Principal Engineer role is a director-level strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise and a record of influencing product and platform direction, and the resume reflects a strong senior/staff implementer rather than a strategic technical leader. The gap is substantial enough that a strong recommendation is not warranted, though the AI-agent infrastructure work makes a screening conversation defensible.
